headline: A Crypto Casino's Viral Promise Funds a Grandson's Dream Trip
meta_description: Jacob Allmendinger spent his house deposit to take his 80-year-old grandfather to the World Cup. A company's viral promise paid it all back.
summary_short: A 21-year-old's sacrifice to share the World Cup with his grandfather inspired an unexpected gift of $13,000—turning a financial gamble into a life-changing moment.
body:
Jacob Allmendinger made a choice that surprised even himself. The 21-year-old drained his entire house deposit—$13,000—to take his grandfather Geoff Golliker, 80, to the World Cup in the USA and Mexico. For years, Geoff had taken Jacob to matches in their hometown of Hull. Now it was time to return the favor.

What happened next defied the odds. When their story went viral on social media, a US-based crypto casino called Metawin noticed. The company made an audacious pledge: they'd reimburse the money if England beat Panama by more than two goals. Jacob was skeptical—understandably.

But after England's 2-0 victory, Metawin sweetened the offer, promising £10,000 if England won against DR Congo by any score. "I didn't quite believe it, to be honest, until I looked at my bank and it was there," Jacob told the BBC. "I was just in shock."

The pair have already witnessed unforgettable moments: matches in New York and Atlanta, and England's historic 3-2 win at the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City. The viral attention has touched something universal—people sharing messages about lost grandparents and missed opportunities.

For Jacob, the money represents possibility. "I could buy a house, anything is possible," he said. Yet perhaps the real treasure is simpler: the time spent with his best mate who happens to be his grandfather.
